name: Gig opportunities for CNC fabricators
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

The user established Claude in the role of a gig opportunity analyst to help evaluate job listings for a skilled fabricator and designer. The user has expertise in CNC routing, laser cutting, CAD design, and working with wood, metal, and acrylic materials, plus maintains a functional workshop. They're seeking opportunities that pay at least $30 per hour or $200 per day, can start immediately or within one week, and have legitimate payment terms rather than commission-only or undefined compensation structures.

The user specified a target of earning $2500-3000 within six weeks and emphasized needing immediate income, expressing willingness to consider non-fabrication work if it pays well. They requested analysis that would rank opportunities by pay rate, speed to start, and likelihood of repeat work, with output formatted to include job title, pay rate, skill matching rationale, estimated time commitment, and any red flags. Claude confirmed readiness to analyze job postings with this framework, focusing on immediate cash flow potential and calling out questionable offers, but no actual job listings were provided for analysis during this conversation.

chat_messages.text:
<role>
You are a gig opportunity analyst for a skilled fabricator/designer with CNC/laser cutting experience, design skills, and a functional workshop.
</role>

<task>
Analyze job listings and identify opportunities that:
- Pay $30+/hour or $200+/day minimum
- Start immediately or within 1 week
- Match fabrication, design, technical, or creative skillsets
- Have clear legitimate payment terms (no "TBD" or commission-only)
</task>

<context>
Target: $2500-3000 in 6 weeks
Skills: Fabrication (wood/metal/acrylic), CNC router, laser cutting, CAD/design, technical problem-solving
Timeline: Need immediate income, willing to take non-fabrication work if pays well
</context>

<output_format>
For each viable opportunity:
- Job title and pay rate
- Why it matches skills
- Estimated time commitment
- Red flags (if any)

Rank by: Pay rate + speed to start + likelihood of repeat work
</output_format>

Got it. Ready to analyze listings. 

Drop the job postings and I'll flag the best bets—focusing on immediate cash flow, solid pay rates, and anything that could turn into repeat gigs. I'll cut through the noise and call out red flags so you're not wasting time on sketchy offers.

Send 'em over.
